title: psychotria costivenia subsp. altorum
intro: Psychotria costivenia subsp. altorum, also known as the forest mint, is a species of plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of forest mint include its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Forest mint can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
